title: PV Calculation Engine
copyright: © 2026 NexVigilant, LLC. Signal half-life model, NexVigilant QBR Framework, and composite scoring methodology are proprietary. Standard PV algorithms (PRR, ROR, IC, EBGM, Naranjo, WHO-UMC) follow published methodologies.
description: Pharmacovigilance computation tools: signal detection (PRR/ROR/IC/EBGM), causality assessment (Naranjo, WHO-UMC), seriousness classification (ICH E2A), benefit-risk analysis, and reporting rate calculations. Standard algorithms follow published WHO, FDA, and ICH methodologies. Composite scoring and signal half-life models are proprietary NexVigilant methods.

tools.name: compute-prr
tools.description: Compute Proportional Reporting Ratio (PRR) for a drug-event pair from a 2x2 contingency table. PRR > 2 with chi-squared > 4 and N >= 3 indicates a signal.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: Reports of target drug WITH target event

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: Reports of target drug WITHOUT target event

parameters.name: c
parameters.description: Reports of other drugs WITH target event

parameters.name: d
parameters.description: Reports of other drugs WITHOUT target event

result.description: PRR value, chi-squared, 95% CI, signal determination

tools.name: compute-ror
tools.description: Compute Reporting Odds Ratio (ROR) for a drug-event pair from a 2x2 contingency table. ROR > 1 with lower 95% CI > 1 indicates a signal.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: Reports of target drug WITH target event

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: Reports of target drug WITHOUT target event

parameters.name: c
parameters.description: Reports of other drugs WITH target event

parameters.name: d
parameters.description: Reports of other drugs WITHOUT target event

result.description: ROR value, 95% CI bounds, signal determination

method.description: Reporting Odds Ratio with log-normal CI

tools.name: compute-ic
tools.description: Compute Information Component (IC/BCPNN) for a drug-event pair. IC > 0 with IC025 (lower credibility interval) > 0 indicates a signal. Bayesian method used by WHO-UMC.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: Reports of target drug WITH target event

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: Reports of target drug WITHOUT target event

parameters.name: c
parameters.description: Reports of other drugs WITH target event

parameters.name: d
parameters.description: Reports of other drugs WITHOUT target event

result.description: IC value, IC025/IC975 credibility interval, signal determination

tools.name: compute-ebgm
tools.description: Compute Empirical Bayesian Geometric Mean (EBGM/GPS) for a drug-event pair. EBGM > 2 with EB05 (lower bound) > 1 indicates a signal. FDA method.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: Reports of target drug WITH target event

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: Reports of target drug WITHOUT target event

parameters.name: c
parameters.description: Reports of other drugs WITH target event

parameters.name: d
parameters.description: Reports of other drugs WITHOUT target event

result.description: EBGM value, EB05/EB95 bounds, signal determination

tools.name: assess-naranjo-causality
tools.description: Compute Naranjo adverse drug reaction probability score (0-13). Categories: Definite (>=9), Probable (5-8), Possible (1-4), Doubtful (<=0).

parameters.name: previous_reports
parameters.description: Are there previous conclusive reports on this reaction?

parameters.name: after_drug
parameters.description: Did the adverse event appear after the suspected drug was administered?

parameters.name: improved_on_withdrawal
parameters.description: Did the reaction improve when the drug was discontinued? (yes/no/not_withdrawn)

parameters.name: reappeared_on_rechallenge
parameters.description: Did the reaction reappear on rechallenge? (yes/no/not_done)

parameters.name: alternative_causes
parameters.description: Are there alternative causes that could have caused the reaction?

parameters.name: placebo_reaction
parameters.description: Did the reaction appear when placebo was given? (yes/no/not_done)

parameters.name: drug_detected
parameters.description: Was the drug detected in blood/fluids in toxic concentrations? (yes/no/not_done)

parameters.name: dose_related
parameters.description: Was the reaction more severe with higher dose or less severe with lower dose? (yes/no/not_done)

parameters.name: previous_exposure
parameters.description: Did the patient have a similar reaction to the same or similar drugs previously?

parameters.name: objective_evidence
parameters.description: Was the adverse event confirmed by objective evidence?

tools.name: classify-seriousness
tools.description: Classify adverse event seriousness per ICH E2A criteria. An event is serious if it meets ANY criterion: death, life-threatening, hospitalization, disability, congenital anomaly, or medically important.

parameters.name: resulted_in_death
parameters.description: Did the event result in death?

parameters.name: life_threatening
parameters.description: Was the event life-threatening at the time of occurrence?

parameters.name: required_hospitalization
parameters.description: Did the event require or prolong hospitalization?

parameters.name: resulted_in_disability
parameters.description: Did the event result in persistent or significant disability?

parameters.name: congenital_anomaly
parameters.description: Did the event result in a congenital anomaly/birth defect?

parameters.name: medically_important
parameters.description: Is the event medically important based on clinical judgment?

result.description: Classification (serious/non-serious), criteria met, severity rank, reporting timeline

method.description: ICH E2A Clinical Safety Data Management

tools.name: compute-benefit-risk
tools.description: Compute a quantitative benefit-risk ratio using the NexVigilant QBR framework. Scores benefit (efficacy × population impact) against risk (severity × frequency × detectability).

parameters.name: efficacy_score
parameters.description: Drug efficacy score (0.0-1.0)

parameters.name: population_impact
parameters.description: Fraction of population that benefits (0.0-1.0)

parameters.name: risk_severity
parameters.description: Severity of the primary risk (0.0-1.0, where 1.0 = fatal)

parameters.name: risk_frequency
parameters.description: Frequency of the primary risk (0.0-1.0)

parameters.name: risk_detectability
parameters.description: How detectable is the risk before harm (0.0-1.0, where 1.0 = easily detected)

result.description: Benefit score, risk score, B/R ratio, classification (favorable/unfavorable/marginal)

tools.name: compute-disproportionality-table
tools.description: Compute ALL four disproportionality measures (PRR, ROR, IC, EBGM) simultaneously from a single 2x2 contingency table. Returns all scores with signal thresholds.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: Reports of target drug WITH target event

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: Reports of target drug WITHOUT target event

parameters.name: c
parameters.description: Reports of other drugs WITH target event

parameters.name: d
parameters.description: Reports of other drugs WITHOUT target event

result.description: All four scores (PRR, ROR, IC, EBGM) with CIs and consensus signal

tools.name: assess-who-umc-causality
tools.description: Assess drug-event causality using the WHO-UMC system. Categories: Certain, Probable/Likely, Possible, Unlikely, Conditional/Unclassified, Unassessable/Unclassifiable.

parameters.name: temporal_relationship
parameters.description: Is there a reasonable temporal relationship between drug and event?

parameters.name: known_response
parameters.description: Is this a known response pattern to the drug?

parameters.name: dechallenge_positive
parameters.description: Did the event improve on drug withdrawal? (yes/no/not_done)

parameters.name: rechallenge_positive
parameters.description: Did the event recur on re-exposure? (yes/no/not_done)

parameters.name: alternative_explanation
parameters.description: Could the event be explained by disease or other drugs?

parameters.name: sufficient_information
parameters.description: Is there sufficient information to assess?

tools.name: compute-reporting-rate
tools.description: Compute adverse event reporting rate per unit exposure. Normalizes raw case counts by exposure denominator (prescriptions, patient-years, or doses sold).

parameters.name: case_count
parameters.description: Number of adverse event reports

parameters.name: exposure_denominator
parameters.description: Total exposure (prescriptions, patient-years, or doses)

parameters.name: denominator_unit
parameters.description: Unit of denominator: prescriptions, patient_years, or doses

parameters.name: time_period_months
parameters.description: Time period in months over which cases were collected

result.description: Reporting rate, annualized rate, 95% Poisson CI

tools.name: compute-signal-half-life
tools.description: Compute signal persistence using exponential decay model. Estimates how long a safety signal remains actionable based on initial strength and decay rate.

parameters.name: initial_signal_strength
parameters.description: Initial signal score (PRR, ROR, or EBGM value)

parameters.name: decay_rate
parameters.description: Monthly decay rate (0.0-1.0, typical: 0.05-0.15)

parameters.name: detection_threshold
parameters.description: Minimum signal value considered actionable (default: 2.0)

result.description: Half-life in months, months to threshold, 6/12/24 month projections

tools.name: compute-expectedness
tools.description: Assess whether an adverse event is expected (listed) or unexpected (unlisted) based on reference safety information. Unexpected serious events require expedited reporting.

parameters.name: event_term
parameters.description: MedDRA preferred term for the adverse event

parameters.name: drug_name
parameters.description: Drug name to check against

parameters.name: reference_source
parameters.description: Reference: SmPC, USPI, IB, or CCDS

result.description: Expectedness classification, reference source, reporting implications

tools.name: compute-time-to-onset
tools.description: Analyze time-to-onset patterns using Weibull distribution. Shape parameter k indicates: k<1 early hazard (direct pharmacological), k≈1 constant hazard (idiosyncratic), k>1 late hazard (accumulation/sensitization).

parameters.name: onset_days
parameters.description: Array of onset times in days after drug exposure (at least 3 positive values)

tools.name: score-case-completeness
tools.description: Score ICSR completeness against E2B(R3) minimum data elements. Checks required fields (patient, reporter, drug, event) and recommended fields (age, sex, dates, outcome, causality).

parameters.name: patient_identifier
parameters.description: Patient identifier (initials, number, or DOB)

parameters.name: reporter_identifier
parameters.description: Reporter identifier (name or initials)

parameters.name: suspect_drug
parameters.description: Suspect drug name

parameters.name: adverse_event
parameters.description: Adverse event description

parameters.name: patient_age
parameters.description: Patient age or age group

parameters.name: patient_sex
parameters.description: Patient sex

parameters.name: event_onset_date
parameters.description: Date of event onset

parameters.name: drug_start_date
parameters.description: Date drug therapy started

parameters.name: drug_indication
parameters.description: Indication for drug use

parameters.name: event_outcome
parameters.description: Outcome of the event

parameters.name: reporter_country
parameters.description: Country of reporter

parameters.name: report_type
parameters.description: Report type (spontaneous, study, etc.)

parameters.name: seriousness_criteria
parameters.description: Seriousness assessment

parameters.name: causality_assessment
parameters.description: Causality assessment performed

parameters.name: action_taken
parameters.description: Action taken with suspect drug

parameters.name: rechallenge_info
parameters.description: Dechallenge/rechallenge information

tools.name: compute-number-needed-harm
tools.description: Compute Number Needed to Harm (NNH) from exposed and unexposed incidence rates. NNH = 1/ARI where ARI = |risk_exposed - risk_unexposed|. Lower NNH = more frequent harm.

parameters.name: risk_exposed
parameters.description: Incidence rate in exposed group (0.0-1.0)

parameters.name: risk_unexposed
parameters.description: Incidence rate in unexposed group (0.0-1.0)

tools.name: compute-confidence-interval
tools.description: Compute Wilson score confidence interval for a proportion. More reliable than Wald CI for small samples and extreme proportions (near 0 or 1).

parameters.name: successes
parameters.description: Number of successes (events observed)

parameters.name: total
parameters.description: Total number of observations

parameters.name: confidence_level
parameters.description: Confidence level (0.90, 0.95, or 0.99; default 0.95)

tools.name: compute-signal-trend
tools.description: Detect trend direction in time-series safety signal scores using linear regression. Reports slope, R-squared, and whether the signal is increasing, decreasing, or stable.

parameters.name: observations
parameters.description: Array of {period, score} objects representing signal scores over time (at least 2)
